data.title: Merge-safe ticket IDs and concurrent-edit hardening
data.description:
## Context

DSO ticket IDs are generated from the first 8 hex characters of a uuid4 (ticket-create.sh:169-177), yielding only 32 bits of entropy. Birthday-paradox collision probability exceeds 1% at ~9,300 tickets and 50% at ~77,000. Additionally, STATUS events written concurrently on the orphan `tickets` branch across parallel sessions can produce semantic conflicts that surface only at reducer time, and event filenames derived from clock-time-dependent sources can race on git merge. This epic hardens both identity and concurrency without changing observable CLI behavior.

## Success Criteria
- ID scheme collision-safe to >=10^9 tickets (full uuid4 hex or hash-based, optionally salted by env-id). Existing 8-char display form may be retained as a stable short-ref.
- Event filename scheme guarantees uniqueness across parallel sessions (no merge conflicts possible on identical filenames produced by concurrent writers).
- Semantic STATUS conflicts detected and automatically resolved on fetch via reducer replay (optimistic-concurrency CAS semantics preserved).
- Backfill migration rewrites existing IDs without breaking LINK references (target_id values updated atomically within each LINK/UNLINK event chain).
- Zero regressions in bridge (SYNC/jira_key mapping), reducer compiled state, CLI surface, or test suite.

## Approach
Replace the uuid4 truncation in ticket-create.sh with a collision-safe scheme (full hex or blake2 salted by .env-id). Introduce a deterministic event filename suffix (monotonic-per-process counter + env-id) so two concurrent writers on different machines never produce the same filename. Extend reducer conflict handling to replay STATUS conflicts and pick the winner by causal order rather than file mtime. A one-shot migration script rewrites existing IDs and patches all LINK/UNLINK target_id references in-place.

## Dependencies
No upstream dependencies. Additive hardening only.

## Out of Scope
- CLI UX changes (no new commands, no flag changes)
- Reducer compiled-state schema changes beyond ID field widening
- Jira-side ID changes (jira_key unaffected)
